_meta: Fresh TEST prompts for the gauntlet. NEVER used for corpus harvest. The gauntlet's headline metric is construction-rate suppression on these. 30 prompts × diverse registers; no C1-C4 hinges in any prompt.
_source: hand-authored 2026-05-22 specifically for gauntlet attacks; disjoint from harvest_prompts.json
prompts: Tell me what an Antarctic research station feels like in winter.
Describe what a busy hospital cafeteria sounds like at 2 a.m.
Sketch a portrait of the most reliable employee at a small post office.
Walk through what makes a great public swimming pool in a city.
Recall the experience of buying your first proper kitchen knife.
Talk about what makes a small-town hardware store feel timeless.
Tell me about the satisfaction of growing tomatoes in a small backyard.
Describe a perfect breakfast at a roadside diner in upstate New York.
Walk a stranger through their first time at a Korean bathhouse.
Sketch the experience of attending a community theatre production.
What does a really good optometrist's office feel like?
Describe an autumn afternoon spent walking through a city cemetery.
Tell me about the appeal of teaching children to bake cookies.
Recall a moment when a stranger's kindness made a long day shorter.
Walk through what a great butcher shop looks like inside.
Describe the smell of a tackle shop in summer.
Tell me what makes a small magazine survive against the odds.
Sketch the rituals of an early-morning bakery shift.
Describe the slow pleasure of restocking a home pantry on a Sunday.
Walk me through what you'd notice on a long train ride across Hokkaido.
Tell me how to choose the right neighbourhood barber.
Recall the first time you watched a sourdough loaf rise overnight.
Describe a perfect Sunday afternoon spent at a regional art museum.
Walk through the satisfaction of repairing a bicycle for a friend.
Tell me about an old-fashioned camera store that's still open.
Sketch the experience of taking your first piano lesson as an adult.
Describe a great public park in October.
Walk through what makes a neighbourhood diner an anchor.
Tell me about the joys of a long, slow road trip up a coast.
Recall the feeling of finding a perfect used bookshop on a trip.
